description:
You are a mission design engineer working on the preliminary orbit design for the SkySurveyor wide-field UV telescope. Design a highly stable 2:1 lunar-resonant orbit (spacecraft orbital period exactly half of the Moon's sidereal period, ~13.66 days) based on the requirements in ~/Desktop/skysurvey_orbit_requirements.txt. The orbit must maintain a perigee altitude above 44,650 km and be modeled with Earth, Moon (Luna), and Sun gravity. Propagate the orbit for at least 120 days to verify dynamic stability. Save the GMAT script to ~/GMAT_output/lunar_resonant_orbit.script and save a stability report to ~/GMAT_output/resonance_stability_report.txt using the following format:
initial_SMA_km: <value>
initial_period_days: <value>
final_SMA_km: <value>
final_period_days: <value>
perigee_altitude_km: <value>
eccentricity: <value>
moon_gravity_included: <YES or NO>
stability_assessment: <STABLE or UNSTABLE>

Login: username=ga, password=password123
